---
title: "R Language"
subtitle: "Study Group, 6 Verbs, IDEs, Pipes"
author: "Brian Yandell ([_byandell.github.io_](https://byandell.github.io))"
date: last-modified
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    embed-resources: true
    footer: "R Language"
    highlight-style: github
---

# R Digital Tools Contents 

- [Study Group Syllabus](#study-group-syllabus)
- [6 Data Science Verbs](#data-science-verbs)
- [Interactive Development Environments (IDEs)](#interactive-development-environments)
- [Pipes in R](#pipes-in-r)

# Study Group Syllabus {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## R for Teams in the Data Sciences {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Overview & Purpose
- Assumes a basic introduction to data analytics (e.g., [Data Carpentry](http://datacarpentry.org) courses).
- Designed for collaborative teams to use R effectively to address data science problems.
- Housed in the **[UW Madison Data Science Hub GitHub Organization](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience)**, which Brian Yandell helped establish.
- Used in the **[COMBEE R Study Group](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences)** at UW-Madison.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-note}
### Web References
- Repo: [R for Data Sciences](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences)
- Live Web Version: [R for Data Sciences Web](http://www.stat.wisc.edu/~yandell/R_for_data_sciences)
:::
:::
:::

## Broad Syllabus Organization {.compact-slide}

Material is organized into "verb" folders representing key stages of the data science workflow:

::: {.incremental}
- **[curate](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/curate)**: Manage and transform data structures.
- **[visualize](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/visualize)**: Explore data relationships visually.
- **[organize](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/organize)**: Document and structure reproducible workflows.
- **[analyze](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/analyze)**: Fit linear models and run statistical analyses.
- **[profile](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/profile)**: Check efficiency and test code performance.
- **[connect](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/connect)**: Bridge R with other computing environments and databases.
:::

## Study Materials & Indices {.compact-slide}

Additional resources located in the repository root directory:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Core Documentation
- **[overview.md](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/overview.md)**: Index of R materials.
- **[reference.md](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/reference.md)**: Hand-selected external resources.
- **[repositories.md](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/repositories.md)**: Public dataset libraries.
- **[learnR.md](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/learnR.md)**: R syntax tips and tricks.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Background & Legacy
- **[background.md](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/background.md)**: Introductory slides on R history.
- **[Bates.md](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/Bates.md)**: Special teaching index developed by Douglas Bates.
- **`data`**: Local sample folders.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
Many thanks to Douglas Bates and the COMBEE Study Group for co-teaching and sharing the early versions of these materials in 2014–2017.
:::

# 6 Data Science Verbs {background-color="#f0fdf4"}

## [1. Curate: Data Structures & Import](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/curate) {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Tidyverse Curation
- Read, manipulate, and display data summaries in concise tables.
- Work with data frames using **[tidyverse](http://tidyverse.org/)** tools.
- Write cleaned up data tables in CSV/flat formats.
- String manipulation and cleaning using characters/regular expressions.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key Repository Modules
- `tidyverse` / `intro_dplyr` / `data_tables`
- `purrr`: List/vector iteration mapping (`map` & `transpose`).
- `species`: Integrated `dplyr`, `tidyr`, and `purrr` workflow.
- `string` / `regex` / `file`
:::
:::

## [2. Visualize: Grammar of Graphics](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/visualize) {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Graphical Explorations
- Understand key components of the **grammar of graphics**.
- Visualize data relationships with the **[ggplot2](http://ggplot2.org/)** package.
- Examine formatting/layout packages (`cowplot` / `GGally`).
- Create network observations in connected graphs (`network`).
- Share results on the web with interactive **[shiny](shiny.Rmd)** apps.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key Repository Modules
- `surveys_ggplot.R`
- `ggplot2.Rmd`
- `visualize.md`
- `graphics.md`
- `shiny.Rmd`
:::
:::

## [3. Organize: Workflows & Databases](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/organize) {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Structure & Reproducibility
- Document ongoing research with **[R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com/)** reports.
- Collapse repeated code blocks into reusable **[functions](function.Rmd)**.
- Use **[git and GitHub](http://happygitwithr.com/)** for version control.
- Organize documentation and tasks into shared **[packages](package.Rmd)**.
- Manage external databases using `SQLite`, `DBI`, `dbplyr`, `RDS`, or `feather` formats.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key Repository Modules
- `Rmarkdown.md` / `RmarkdownExample.Rmd`
- `function.Rmd`
- `package.Rmd`
- `github.md`
- `database.md` / `SQLiteDplyr.Rmd`
- `writing.md`
:::
:::

## [4. Analyze: Models & Formulas](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/analyze) {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Statistical Foundations
- Correlate measurements and compare across groups using **linear models** (`lm`).
- Specify layout matrices and regression variables using R **formulas**.
- Organize model parameters cleanly using **`broom`**, `car`, or `emmeans` packages.
- Study GLMs, non-linear models, and hierarchical systems genetics.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key Repository Modules
- `linear_model.Rmd`
- `Formulas.Rmd`
- `correlate.Rmd`
- `covary.Rmd`
- `sysgen.md`
:::
:::

## [5. Profile: Optimization & Simulation](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/profile) {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Performance & Testing
- Profile code to identify speed bottlenecks using `Rprof` and **`profvis`**.
- Debug code using `traceback`, interactive `browser` sessions, and RMarkdown breakpoints.
- Simulate data to study statistical properties (fitting speed, simulations).
- Create diagnostic plots to test statistical model assumptions.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key Repository Modules
- `profile.Rmd` / `lineprof.Rmd`
- `simulate.Rmd`: Simulation studies (Doug Bates).
- `SimSpeed.Rmd` / `LmSimulation.Rmd`
:::
:::

## [6. Connect: Interoperability](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/tree/master/connect) {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Beyond R
- Use the **Unix/Linux shell** to search, edit, and organize projects.
- Build automated, reproducible pipelines using **GNU Make**.
- Scale up workflows and the split-apply-combine paradigm using remote clusters.
- Create reproducible, isolated containers of software using **Docker** and Guix.
- Typesetting scientific articles using **LaTeX**.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key Repository Modules
- `linux.md` / `beyondR.md`
- `docker.md`
- `latex.md`
- `reproducible.md`
- `scaling_up.md`
:::
:::

# Interactive Development Environments {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Using R in VS Code {.compact-slide}

VS Code has become a highly customizable IDE for R programming, matching RStudio functions:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Interactive VS Code Setup
- Install the VS Code R extension to execute lines of code and preview datasets.
- Set up markdown engines for inline output rendering.
- Configure customized code chunk snippets to improve keyboard speed.
- Bind keyboard shortcuts to quickly insert RMarkdown/Quarto code chunks.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Key Guides
- **[R in Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/languages/r)** (VS Code docs).
- **[Interactive R Programming](https://www.datanovia.com/books/r-in-vscode/)** (Datanovia).
- **[Setup VS Code to run R](https://www.r-bloggers.com/2021/01/setup-visual-studio-code-to-run-r-on-vscode-2021/)** (R-Bloggers).
- **[Snippet Key Bindings](https://www.schmidtynotes.com/blog/r/2021-09-28-vscode-rmd-code-chunk-snippet/)** (Schmidty Notes).
:::
:::

## Radian: A Modern Console for R {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Features of Radian
- **[Radian](https://github.com/randy3k/radian)** is a modern console alternative for R.
- Built on Python's `prompt_toolkit` to provide:
  - Syntax highlighting.
  - Multi-line editing and completion.
  - Bracketed pasting (copying whole code blocks).
  - Searchable command history.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.callout-warning}
### AI Environments Note
Radian is not always seamless within terminal-based AI environments (like cursor or terminal agents). It can interfere with the interactive memory or command execution of these tools. Use **Posit's RStudio** if debugging issues.
:::
:::
:::

- Learn more at [radian: a modern console for R](https://fromthebottomoftheheap.net/2019/06/18/radian-console-for-r/).

## Radian VS Code Configuration {.compact-slide}

Add the following settings to your VS Code `settings.json` and system path:

```json
// Define binary path based on OS
"r.rterm.mac": "/usr/local/bin/radian",
"r.rterm.linux": "/usr/local/bin/radian",
"r.rterm.windows": "C:\\Users\\<username>\\AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Python\\Python310\\Scripts\\radian.exe",

// Set console behaviors
"r.bracketedPast": true,
"r.alwaysUseActiveTerminal": true, // Run code (CTRL + ENTER) in current terminal
"r.rterm.option": [
    "--no-save",
    "--no-restore",
    "--quiet",
    "--r-binary=/usr/local/bin/radian"
],
"r.sessionWatcher": true,
"r.sessionWatcher.showSavePrompt": false
```

In your `.Rprofile` home file, disable automatic completion-on-type to prevent latency:
```R
options(radian.complete_while_typing = FALSE)
```

# Pipes in R {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Pipes: Native `|>` vs Magrittr `%>%` {.compact-slide}

Pipes chain multiple nested function calls into sequential, readable steps. 

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### magrittr Pipe (`%>%`)
- Loaded via `magrittr` or `dplyr`.
- Rewrites the AST via function wrappers.
- Allows placing arguments anywhere using the dot `.` placeholder.
```R
# magrittr style
df %>% filter(x > 10)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Native Pipe (`|>`)
- Built directly into R base (version 4.1+).
- Zero packages needed, no function call overhead (compiled on parse).
- Requires using the underscore `_` placeholder (introduced in R 4.2+).
```R
# Native style
df |> filter(x > 10)
```
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note}
### Key Resource
Check the tidyverse blog post [Base R (4.1+) |> vs magrittr %>% pipe](https://tidyverse.org/blog/2023/04/base-vs-magrittr-pipe/) for detailed performance differences.
:::

## Pipe Comparison Matrix {.compact-slide}

| Feature | magrittr Pipe (`%>%`) | Native Pipe (`|>`) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Availability** | Requires `library(magrittr)` or `tidyverse` | Built-in base R since version 4.1 |
| **Execution Speed** | Slower (function wrapper wrapper) | Faster (parsed directly to standard calls) |
| **Placeholder** | Support for `.` placeholder anywhere | Support for `_` placeholder (R 4.2+, named args only) |
| **Syntax Errors** | Caught during runtime | Caught during parsing |
| **Debugging** | Deep nested stack traces | Standard simple stack traces |

```R
# Placeholder comparison:
# magrittr:
mtcars %>% lm(mpg ~ cyl, data = .)

# Native R (4.2+):
mtcars |> lm(mpg ~ cyl, data = _)
```
